steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for the function of the Grenada information in the passage. Grenada opens the text as an example of a fuel-use shift. The passage then introduces the energy ladder as the standard explanation for such shifts, and finally cites a study showing that model is "reductive." The correct answer must capture Grenada's role within that three-part structure.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'poorly suited for evaluating whether the energy ladder is viable': The passage never says Grenada is hard to evaluate — it is offered as a typical example, not an exceptional one that resists analysis. ✗
  • Choice B — 'two different ways that are equally compelling': The passage presents only one explanation (the energy ladder) and then critiques it. There is no second equally compelling explanation introduced. ✗
  • Choice C — 'frequently but inadequately accounted for by the energy ladder': Grenada illustrates the kind of shift the energy ladder "often" explains (frequently) but that the Kenya study shows is reductive (inadequately). Both qualifiers align precisely with the passage. ✓
  • Choice D — 'similar cause as a seemingly unrelated trend in Kenya': The passage does not claim Grenada and Kenya share a cause — the Kenya study actually shows the energy-ladder explanation is oversimplified. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
